methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Humanoid, a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Artem Sokolov, has raised $50M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a broader robotics and manufacturing comp universe spanning 1 public company and 8 private peers including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation approach. Using total capital raised of $50M as a proxy for seed-stage value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement yields $125M. This method directly reflects anticipated value accretion from the subsequent financing round. The Peer-Median approach was not applicable due to insufficient comparable data at the same or adjacent stage; while 11 robotics and manufacturing peers exist in the broader database, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints in the narrow same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier filters needed for robust benchmarking.

The triangulated valuation range is $106M (low) to $144M (high), with a median of $125M. This range reflects a ±15% variance around the stage-progression midpoint, accounting for typical early-stage valuation volatility. Key uncertainties include the absence of direct same-stage peer validation and reliance on a single methodological input. Additional near-term fundraising activity, competitive positioning relative to the named peer set, and underlying unit economics remain material variables not captured in this quantitative framework.
